'''Doolb Snoil''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Vippit]] barrister who served the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the waning years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Biography==
Born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]], his grandfather had been a diplomat who was involved in negotiations with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and his father was presumably also involved in legal work. Snoil had attended a legal university on [[Mrlsst/Legends|Mrlsst]]. He had then become an apprentice in the [[Gevarno Cluster/Legends|Gevarno Cluster]]. By the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], he had developed a reputation for having one of the top three or four legal minds in the Republic, and was a member of the [[Coruscant College of Law]].

Snoil met [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] when the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] saved him from a clan rebellion on [[Rijel-12|Rijel-Twelve]] in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]]. As a result Snoil could not mate because Kenobi might have requested his help: no respectable Vippit female would enter into negotiations with one who might be called off by a third party without warning.

Around [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Snoil was assigned to assist his friend Kenobi, who was now a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] on the rise of becoming a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]], with negotiations between the Galactic Republic and [[Five Families of Cestus Cybernetics|Five Families]] of [[Cestus Cybernetics]] on [[Ord Cestus/Legends|Ord Cestus]] concerning the production and sale of new [[JK-13 security droid|Jedi killing droids]]. However, when saving Kenobi from a rogue droid, a stalactite fell on him from a great height and killed him.

==Personality and traits==
Snoil was a very diligent and loyal asset to both the Republic and Kenobi, working thoroughly and tirelessly night after night, often without sleep. He enjoyed decorating his shell with [[viptiel]]s.

==Behind the scenes==
Doolb Snoil was first mentioned in [[Steven Barnes]]' short story ''[[The Hive]]'',<ref name="Hive">''[[The Hive]]''</ref> released as an eBook in [[2004]]<ref name="ReleaseTheHive">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/8832/the-hive-star-wars-legends-short-story-by-steven-barnes/9780345478689|text=The Hive: Star Wars Legends (Short Story)}}</ref> prior to it's inclusion in the paperback version of his<ref name="TCD">''[[The Cestus Deception]]''</ref> [[2005]]<ref name="TCDDate">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/8831/the-cestus-deception-star-wars-legends-clone-wars-by-steven-barnes|text=The Cestus Deception: Star Wars Legends (Clone Wars)}}</ref> novel ''[[The Cestus Deception]]''. Barnes created Snoil's name by reversing the title of one of his other novels, [[Wikipedia:Lion's Blood|''Lion's Blood'']].<ref name="EssentialReader">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref>
